# Where Retail Edge Lives (and Doesn't) in Crypto Spot Trading: Evidence from a Production System, 2021–2026

**HYDRA Research · July 2026 · v1.0**

*All results generated by the open HYDRA backtest platform (engine-verbatim
replay, realistic post-only fills, 16 bps maker fees) on Kraken 1h OHLC
tape ending 2026-07-11. Raw evidence in `research/data/`; every table is
regenerable from `hydra_history.sqlite` via the study scripts described in
§2. Not financial advice.*

---

## Abstract

We report five controlled studies run on a production spot-trading system
(HYDRA v2.28.0) against 1–13 years of real Kraken tape. Findings: (1) a
representative retail technical strategy set (regime-switched momentum /
mean-reversion / grid at 15m–1h horizons) has **no after-fee edge** and
underperforms buy-and-hold in bear markets; (2) a cross-pair "triangle"
structure (SOL/BTC bridge) adds **zero trades or negative value** — its
apparent benefit is cash dilution; (3) a **daily trend-ensemble overlay**
(sma200 / ema20×100 / don55) dominates the raw system in all six tested
windows, converting −0.4 to −0.5% yearly bleed into ≈0 to +1% with ≤1.9%
max drawdown; (4) entering on the daily signal alone **fails** when
composed with intraday defensive exits (−5.4% vs +0.1%, 2y) — exit-system
composition, not signal quality, was the binding failure mode; (5) the
drawdown circuit-breaker threshold is **immaterial** to a risk-managed
configuration and acts purely as a ~1:1 loss floor on an unmanaged one —
no threshold converts a losing cycle into a profitable one. Over the
bear year ending 2026-07-11 the final system returned **+0.04% (12/12
non-negative months, 0.05% max DD) versus −48.4% for 50/50 buy-and-hold**.
We conclude that retail-accessible edge in US crypto spot markets is
limited to *risk-managed cyclical beta* — capital preservation in
winters plus systematic re-entry — and that the profitable strategy
classes visible in this market (market making, funding/basis carry,
short-side expression) are structurally unavailable to a US retail
spot-only account.

---

## 1. System under test

HYDRA is a regime-adaptive Kraken spot agent: pure-Python indicator
engine (Wilder RSI/ATR, population-variance Bollinger, MACD), four
strategies switched by an EMA20/50 + adaptive-volatility regime
detector, quarter/half-Kelly sizing, limit post-only execution, 15%
drawdown circuit breaker, an optional 3-model LLM review layer, and
eleven deterministic guardrail rules. v2.27 added hold-through rails
(TREND_UP-only entries ≥0.65 confidence, forced flattening in
TREND_DOWN) and a friction-expectancy gate. v2.28 added the daily
trend-ensemble overlay evaluated here.

## 2. Methodology

All studies replay real Kraken 1h OHLC through the **live engine code**
(no reimplementation) with next-bar realistic post-only fills and 16 bps
maker fees per side. Studies are paired A/B runs differing in exactly
one environment flag, executed in isolated subprocesses. Windows are 1y,
2y, and 3y ending 2026-07-11 on SOL/USD + BTC/USD ($1,000 per pair)
unless noted. Two platform defects were repaired *before* gating so the
gates would be honest: per-trade realized profit was not recorded
(silently disabling Monte Carlo / bootstrap resampling) and walk-forward
windows collapsed to identical slices at default parameters (reporting
zero variance as fake stability).

**Decision rule ("the gate"):** a change ships default-ON only if it
improves return without materially worsening drawdown in *every* tested
window; a change that fails any window is rejected and removed.

## 3. Results

### 3.1 The retail technical core has no after-fee edge

One-year replay of the pre-rails engine (15m candles, competition
sizing) — `data/validation_results.json`:

| Pair | Strategy | Buy & hold | Strategy maxDD |
|---|---|---|---|
| SOL/USD | **−54.6%** (Sharpe −1.12) | −44.5% | 67.7% |
| BTC/USD | **−28.5%** (Sharpe −0.90) | −25.5% | 46.2% |

The strategy set *added* losses to a losing market. This is consistent
with the arithmetic of the venue: a 15m–1h signal must repeatedly clear
~0.42–0.6% round-trip friction (2×16 bps + spread) that accrues to
market makers and the exchange regardless of direction.

### 3.2 The cross-pair bridge only hurts (`data/bridge_isolation.json`)

| Config (2y) | Return | Sharpe |
|---|---|---|
| Triangle (with SOL/BTC) | −0.58% | **−0.99** |
| No bridge | −0.52% | **−0.63** |
| Bridge alone | −0.68% (1 trade) | −1.35 |

In 1y the bridge produced **zero trades**; the triangle's better 1y
headline (−0.26% vs −0.39%) is an idle third sleeve diluting the loss.
Coordinator on/off changed nothing. The bridge is now signal-input-only.

### 3.3 The daily trend-ensemble overlay dominates (`data/trend_overlay_gate.json`)

Overlay: score = 0.4·(close>sma200) + 0.4·(ema20>ema100) +
0.2·donchian(55-in/20-out), on daily closes; entries additionally
require score ≥ 0.6; open positions flatten when the ensemble flips;
entries are vol-targeted (30% annualized / realized, capped at 1).
Antecedent evidence: the same ensemble on daily bars carries BTC 11y
Sharpe 1.36–1.50 at ~30% maxDD vs buy-and-hold 1.13 at 84%
(`data/trend_results.json`).

| Window | Overlay ON | Overlay OFF |
|---|---|---|
| 1y | **+0.01%** / Sharpe 0.46 / DD 0.02% | −0.39% / −1.33 / 0.43% |
| 2y | **+0.01%** / 0.03 / 0.25% | −0.52% / −0.63 / 0.93% |
| 3y | **+0.30%** / 0.37 / 0.52% | +0.22% / 0.09 / 1.83% |

Six of six comparisons favor ON. Conviction sizing (allocate
vol-target × position cap when the ensemble confirms, Kelly as floor)
then improved return in 3/3 windows (3y: **+1.00% vs +0.30%**, maxDD
1.87%) — `data/conviction_sizing_gate.json`.

### 3.4 A negative result: daily entries + intraday exits whipsaw (`data/trend_entry_gate.json`)

Entering on the daily ensemble alone (rather than waiting for intraday
confirmation) **failed every window**: −1.55%/−5.37%/−4.16% vs
+0.04%/+0.09%/+1.00%, with 5–10× the trade count. Mechanism: the 1h
defensive flatten exits positions the daily signal immediately re-opens
— a sell-low/buy-high loop. The validated stand-alone daily systems
exit *only* on the daily flip; grafting daily entries onto a faster
exit system destroys them. **Exit-system composition, not signal
quality, is the failure mode.** The path was removed from the codebase.

### 3.5 Drawdown-breaker thresholds cannot create profit (`data/cb_threshold_sweep.json`)

Sweeping the circuit breaker from 5% to 1000% (2y window):

- **Risk-managed config:** identical results at every threshold (maxDD
  1.05% never approaches any of them). The breaker is a dormant backstop.
- **Unmanaged config (rails off):** realized loss ≈ −threshold, ~1:1
  (cb5 → −6.2%, cb10 → −9.8%, cb15 → −15.3%, cb20 → −20.0%). The
  breaker sets *how much you lose*, never *whether you win*.

### 3.6 One-year benchmark (`data/monthly_roi_1y.json`)

Year ending 2026-07-11: HYDRA **+0.04%**, 12/12 non-negative months,
max drawdown 0.05%, one round trip — versus SOL −52.3%, BTC −44.6%,
50/50 −48.4%. The entire edge was *not holding*: +48pp of capital
preservation, delivered by the overlay's flat stance.

## 4. Market context

The year was not anomalous for crypto: from the same tape, BTC calendar
years read +506, −57, +35, +124, +1345, −74, +94, +304, +59, −64, +156,
+121, −6, −29 (2013→2026 YTD) — this is the fourth cyclical bear
(2014/2018/2022/2025-26) and, at −43% rolling-1y, so far the mildest.
31% of all rolling 1y BTC windows (59% for SOL) are negative. Nor was
the broader climate hostile: H1 2026 saw the S&P 500 +9.6% at record
highs, Nasdaq +11%, small caps +22%, while gold fell 7% — the loss was
crypto-idiosyncratic (record ETF outflows, leverage unwind, cycle
psychology), not systemic.

## 5. Discussion: who makes money in this market, and why we didn't

The persistent money-makers in crypto occupy positions a US retail
spot account structurally cannot:

1. **Market makers / HFT** — earn the spread and maker rebates; they
   are the counterparty to every retail taker. Our 16 bps × 2 friction
   is their revenue.
2. **Exchanges and infrastructure** — fee revenue regardless of
   direction.
3. **Funding/basis carry desks** — delta-neutral yield harvesting
   requires derivatives; US retail perpetual access is restricted, and
   HYDRA is spot-only by design.
4. **Short-side expression in bears** — unavailable spot-only.
5. **Full-cycle holders** — long-run BTC beta has been enormous, but
   requires surviving −75–90% drawdowns; most do not.
6. **Issuers/insiders** — allocation and information asymmetries.

The strategy class we began with — directional technical trading on
public signals at intraday horizons — is the class that systematically
*funds* groups 1–2. Our own §3.1 measurement is that transfer, made
legible. What survived gating is the only edge the venue offers this
account: **risk-managed cyclical beta** — hold nothing through winters
(earning the risk-free rate), re-enter systematically when the daily
regime turns. In a bear year its correct output is ≈0%, which is what
it produced.

**On exchange-side carry (verified 2026-07-13, live account query):**
Kraken Earn offers this US account USDC at 1.75% APR *not allocatable*,
no USD strategy at all, BTC at 0.02%, and SOL at 2.89% (instant) /
5.78% (bonded). With T-bills near 4–5%, the cash reserve's yield floor
must live **off-exchange**; only the trading float belongs on-venue.

**Does a US investor need crypto exposure?** Need — no. The investable
case is a small (1–5%) satellite of cyclical growth optionality, which
is also how TradFi has operationalized it (spot ETFs deliver demanded
beta; allocation models, not alpha claims). If held at all, the
full-cycle evidence favors holding it *risk-managed* — the difference
between −48% and 0% this year — with core capital in broad equity
index funds, which returned more in six months of 2026 than this entire
strategy class returned in three years.

## 6. Limitations

Single venue, two pairs, one bear-dominated evaluation window for the
final configuration; the overlay's absolute-return case rests on
cross-cycle evidence (`data/trend_results.json`) rather than live
history; fills are simulated (post-only, next-bar) though fee-true;
May 2026 has a 45-day 1h tape gap (REST depth limit) bridged by daily
bars; results are gross of taxes (though the final system's ~1 trade/yr
churn minimizes them); no statistical significance is claimed for
single-window comparisons — the gate demands unanimity across windows
precisely because per-window inference is weak.

## 7. Future work

Live validation of `--pairs auto` breadth; off-exchange yield-floor
integration for the cash reserve; next bull-leg capture measurement
(the overlay's first live long regime will be its real test);
walk-forward + Monte Carlo re-runs each release now that both are
functional; satellite-asset (e.g. NIGHT/USD) behavior under the R10
coverage contract.

---

*Reproduction: studies were run via `BacktestRunner` with
`data_source="sqlite"` on `hydra_history.sqlite`, paired subprocesses
toggling `HYDRA_TREND_OVERLAY` / `HYDRA_TREND_CONVICTION_SIZING` /
`HYDRA_TREND_ENTRY` / `HYDRA_HOLD_THROUGH` / `CIRCUIT_BREAKER_PCT`.
Engine, platform, and tests: github.com/eternal-roman/hydra @ v2.28.0.*
